meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no version control (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn to implement user onboarding to mitigate the risks of no version control. An intermediate guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: Without version control, your codebase is a ticking time bomb. By implementing a robust user onboarding flow, you create a controlled environment where user states are serialized and tracked. This ensures that even if you lose local work, your application state remains recoverable through structured, database-backed user data.
content.intro:
Imagine the cold sweat of realizing your latest commit—which doesn't exist because you haven't initialized Git—has just overwritten the last four hours of progress. You are staring at a blank terminal, the code you meticulously crafted now a ghost in the machine. This is the reality of building without version control, a trap that has claimed countless student projects.

But there is a path forward. By treating user onboarding not just as a UI feature, but as a data-persistence layer, you can effectively bypass the fragility of your local environment. We are going to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to build a resilient system that captures user state from day one, ensuring your app survives even when your local environment fails.

steps.title: Architecting the Onboarding Schema
steps.description: Before writing code, define the schema for user progress. This ensures that even if your local files are corrupted, the user's state is safely persisted in the backend database.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SQL schema for a user_onboarding table that tracks step completion, timestamps, and current state progress for a SaaS application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create onboarding migration for PostgreSQL with user_id, step_index, and serialized_data columns'

steps.title: Implementing State Persistence
steps.description: Link your UI components to the database so that every action taken by the user is saved. This creates an audit trail that acts as a makeshift version history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React hook 'useOnboardingState' that automatically saves component state to the database on every change.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'implement database write-through for onboarding flow in src/hooks/useOnboarding.ts'

steps.title: Building the Progressive UI
steps.description: Construct a modular onboarding UI that fetches the last known state from the server, allowing users to resume exactly where they left off.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a multi-step onboarding wizard component that reads the current step from the database and renders the corresponding view.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate OnboardingWizard.tsx component using Tailwind CSS that integrates with the existing API'

steps.title: Error Handling and Recovery
steps.description: Implement a fallback mechanism that checks for local storage vs database state to prevent data loss during network interruptions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a utility function that reconciles local storage state with database state to ensure the latest version always wins.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'add state reconciliation logic in src/utils/sync.ts to resolve conflicts between local and remote state'

steps.title: Finalizing the Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Connect your application to a production environment to ensure your work is no longer trapped on your local machine.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a script to deploy the application to Vercel and verify that the database connection is secure.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'deploy current build to Vercel and run smoke tests on the onboarding flow'

pro_tips: Treat every user input as a potential 'save point'; high-frequency state updates prevent data loss during crashes.
Use JSONB in PostgreSQL for your onboarding state; it allows for schema flexibility as your app evolves without requiring complex migrations.
Always implement a 'hard-reset' button in development that clears local state, forcing your app to pull the source of truth from the database.
If you lack version control, create a 'snapshot' folder manually every hour and zip your project—it is a primitive but effective safety net.
Focus on building 'stateless' components where the UI is purely a reflection of the database state; this makes your app inherently more stable.
content.next_steps: Once you have verified your onboarding flow, immediately initialize a Git repository and push to GitHub. Version control is non-negotiable for professional development.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-ready onboarding flow that serves as a foundation for your portfolio. Stop building in the dark and start engineering with confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
